scope: installable static framework floor, external package proof, browser validation and maintenance correctness

supports: package exports define the public interface
package metadata and dependencies are part of reproducible external consumption
sources.limitation: does not prove the package actually works outside its monorepo

supports: npm pack creates the distributable tarball used for isolated consumer tests
sources.limitation: a successful tarball build does not prove its exports or runtime dependencies are correct

supports: ordinary framework baseline includes dev, build and preview commands
static output is placed in a deployable dist directory
sources.limitation: Astro is a comparison control, not a required Hyper dependency

supports: static routes must be known and generated at build time
route collisions and missing paths are framework correctness concerns
sources.limitation: does not establish Hyper's route model or product value

supports: schema-validated structured content is an ordinary framework capability
sources.limitation: Hyper may use a different authoring model if it remains simpler and externally usable

supports: Node 20 provides a stable built-in test runner suitable for package and CLI tests
sources.limitation: test framework choice does not prove test quality

supports: browser tests should verify user-visible behavior rather than private implementation details
sources.limitation: browser automation cannot replace human review

supports: automated accessibility scans can detect common defects
manual and inclusive testing remain necessary
sources.limitation: an automated zero-violation result is not complete accessibility proof

supports: correct incremental rebuilds require complete dependency declarations
sources.limitation: Hyper's dependency index must still be tested against frozen expected affected sets
